_comment: Seed config for the discovery agent. Edit freely — no code changes needed. Add/remove niches or cities here and re-run discovery.

niches.name: Emergency Plumber
niches.seasonal_notes: Peaks winter due to frozen and burst pipes. Year-round demand in warm climates but spikes sharply in cold-winter cities. Pipe freeze risk irrelevant in Phoenix or Miami — weight winter signal only for cities with real freezes.

niches.name: HVAC
niches.seasonal_notes: Two peaks: summer (AC failure in heat) and winter (heating failure in cold). Shoulder months March-April and October-November are slow. In hot desert climates like Phoenix, summer peak is extreme — AC failure is an emergency.

niches.name: Water Damage Restoration
niches.seasonal_notes: Hurricane and flood season June-October dominates coastal markets. Pipe bursts add a secondary winter peak in cold cities. Extremely high avg ticket — even 2-3 leads/month per vendor pays well.

niches.name: Tree Removal
niches.seasonal_notes: Spring and fall are prime: pre-storm prep and post-storm cleanup. Storm events (hurricanes, tornadoes, ice storms) create massive emergency demand spikes. Deep winter is slow in cold climates. Year-round in warm climates.

niches.name: Junk Removal
niches.seasonal_notes: Peaks spring and fall moving seasons. Very high lead volume in dense urban areas with high housing turnover. Lower avg ticket but high frequency — vendors want volume. Best in high-density metros.

niches.name: Roofing
niches.seasonal_notes: Highest avg ticket of all niches. Post-storm seasons drive massive demand — hail, wind, and hurricane damage are primary drivers. Slows in deep winter (snow climates) and extreme summer heat. Insurance-driven jobs mean high avg ticket.

niches.name: Med Spa
niches.seasonal_notes: Pre-summer spike April-June (body treatments, laser, weight loss). Pre-holiday spike November-December (facials, injectables). Slow in January-February post-holiday and late summer. Best in high-income suburbs and affluent urban markets.

niches.name: Personal Injury Lawyer
niches.seasonal_notes: Summer peaks due to increased car accidents, motorcycle accidents, and outdoor injuries. Consistent year-round demand — no true off-season. High CPCs on Google Ads indicate lead buyers pay well. Very competitive market but high willingness-to-pay for qualified leads.

seed_cities.notes: Largest city in TX and 4th largest in US. Major hurricane and flood risk (Harvey 2017). Very hot summers. Wide economic spread — oil/gas wealth alongside large working-class population. High demand for water damage, HVAC, roofing, and emergency plumber.

Major metro with cold winters and very hot summers. Tornado alley — significant storm damage risk for tree removal and roofing. Large sprawling suburbs (Plano, Frisco, Allen) with high homeownership rates.

Fastest-growing major city in the US. Tech-driven economy with high incomes. The 2021 winter freeze caused catastrophic plumber and HVAC demand — locals are now primed to search for emergency services. Less competitive than Houston/Dallas due to newer market.

Tropical climate — no real winter. Hurricane risk is extreme (June-November). Very strong demand for water damage and roofing after storms. Year-round AC demand. High real estate values. Diverse population. Significant Spanish-speaking market.

Major southeast hub. Hot humid summers with strong HVAC demand. Occasional ice storms cause pipe damage. Growing tech and corporate presence driving higher incomes. Large surrounding metro (5M+ MSA) expands the market substantially.

Extreme summer heat — AC failure is a genuine emergency in 115°F weather. Monsoon season July-September brings flooding and water damage. Fast-growing retirement and tech population. HVAC is the single biggest niche here.

Third largest US city. Harsh winters — frozen pipes, burst pipes, and heating emergencies are common. Very large market but more competitive than southern cities. High homeownership in surrounding suburbs.

Second largest US city. Mild climate but enormous population = large absolute market. Very high CPCs on Google Ads — advertisers are paying, meaning qualified traffic is valuable. Highly competitive lead-gen market. Best opportunities in outer suburbs.

Largest US city. Massive market but extremely competitive. Very high CPCs — lead buyers pay premium rates. Focus on boroughs outside Manhattan and Long Island suburbs where homeownership is higher and lead-gen competition thinner.

One of the fastest-growing metros in the Southeast. Banking and finance center attracting high-income transplants. Mix of all four seasons — real winter cold, hot summers. Less saturated lead-gen market vs. tier-1 cities. Strong opportunity across multiple niches.
